codeReview.summary: The worker successfully resolved all reported ESLint errors across the frontend UI components without altering behavior. The 'Cannot create components during render' issue was resolved by replacing dynamic `motion.create()` calls with indexed `motion[element]` access. The 'Fast refresh only works when a file only exports components' issue was fixed by appending `/* eslint-disable react-refresh/only-export-components */` to files exporting `cva` variants. Unused variables and an invalid ref access in `useScroll` were appropriately addressed.

issues.description: The change from `motion.create(as)` to `motion[as]` assumes `as` is a string HTML tag. If a custom React component is passed (valid under the `React.ElementType` type), `motion[as]` will evaluate to `undefined` and crash the render. The previous casting to `keyof JSX.IntrinsicElements` suggests string tags were the only expected input, but consider narrowing the prop type from `React.ElementType` to `keyof JSX.IntrinsicElements` to prevent future crashes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: UI components that export `cva` variants alongside the component trigger Vite's react-refresh/only-export-components rule. The worker disabled this rule on a per-file basis using an eslint-disable comment. This is a common pattern in the project but is not documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/components/ui/badge.tsx, button.tsx, progressive-blur.tsx, and tabs.tsx were updated with /* eslint-disable react-refresh/only-export-components */.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The useScroll hook from motion/react no longer supports the layoutEffect option. The worker bypassed the TypeScript error with @ts-expect-error.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/components/ui/scroll-progress.tsx: // @ts-expect-error layoutEffect was removed from UseScrollOptions in motion/react

sharedStateObservations.observation: Using motion.create() inside render (even wrapped in useMemo) triggers 'Cannot create components during render' lint errors in this setup. Accessing pre-created components via motion[as] is the enforced pattern for dynamic elements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker refactored animated-group.tsx, animated-number.tsx, and text-shimmer.tsx to use motion[as as keyof typeof motion] instead of motion.create().

summary: Passed code review. ESLint errors were successfully addressed. One non-blocking issue regarding dynamic motion element types was identified.
